title: Automorphic Forms - Zeta functions of Siegel threefolds
speaker: Gérard Laumon

proof_stages.label: Context and favorable GSp4 case
proof_stages.summary: The speaker situates zeta functions of Siegel threefolds among known GL2 and PEL Shimura-variety results, emphasizing that GSp4 is favorable because its Levi and endoscopic groups reduce to GL1/GL2-type objects and the needed fundamental lemmas are expected or known.

proof_stages.label: Siegel threefolds and coefficient systems
proof_stages.summary: The Shimura variety for GSp4 is described as the moduli space of principally polarized abelian surfaces with level structure, together with its complex uniformization and l-adic local systems arising from irreducible algebraic representations of regular highest weight.

proof_stages.label: Cohomological L-functions and Lefschetz-Hecke numbers
proof_stages.summary: The local L-factor is reformulated through alternating Frobenius traces on compactly supported cohomology, then generalized by inserting finite Hecke correspondences so that the main object to compute is a Lefschetz number for Frobenius composed with a Hecke operator.

proof_stages.label: Automorphic and endoscopic spectral parameters
proof_stages.summary: To state the formula, the talk introduces the relevant discrete-series L-packet at infinity for GSp4, unramified Frobenius eigenvalue parameters in the dual torus, and the unique elliptic endoscopic group H with its GL2-type automorphic data.

proof_stages.label: Main Lefschetz-number formula
proof_stages.summary: Assuming the required fundamental lemmas and transfers, the Lefschetz number is stated as a main automorphic sum over GSp4, an endoscopic main sum over H, and an explicit complementary term built only from GL1 and GL2 automorphic representations and expressible as a Galois trace.

proof_stages.label: Fixed-point formula and Kottwitz stabilization
proof_stages.summary: The proof begins by applying Pink/Fujiwara's form of the Lefschetz fixed-point formula for large Frobenius powers, then uses Kottwitz's description of fixed points and partial stabilization to rewrite the geometric contribution as elliptic trace-formula terms for G and H with explicit test functions.

proof_stages.label: Trace-formula comparison and elimination of extra terms
proof_stages.summary: Arthur's trace formula converts the stabilized geometric terms to spectral sums, and a standard auxiliary-prime/base-change variation followed by taking the Frobenius power very large kills the unwanted weighted and residual terms, leaving the main formula plus a computable complementary contribution.

proof_stages.label: Galois representations as a consequence
proof_stages.summary: For cohomological automorphic representations of GSp4 that are neither CAP nor endoscopic, the formula implies the existence of corresponding four-dimensional Galois representations with Frobenius traces described by the Satake parameters, up to the still-undetermined multiplicities.

examples.name: Modular curves and GL2 Shimura varieties
examples.note: They serve as the already-understood rank-one benchmark against which the higher-dimensional Siegel threefold problem is introduced.
examples.engagement_reason: He cites modular curves and GL2 Shimura varieties as cases whose zeta functions are already more or less known, without doing any computation on them.

examples.name: Flicker's function-field trace-formula trick
examples.note: The function-field analogue is presented as the source of the simplifying idea: vary an auxiliary Hecke function and take a large Frobenius power to force unwanted trace-formula terms to disappear.
